title: Denied - Sealed Record
content:
I must deny access to the records you requested on the basis of Public Officers Law Section 87(2)(a). These records are sealed under court order, pursuant to Criminal Procedure Law Section 160.50 and can only be requested by the arrested person or their representative.

In order to release these records, this unit must receive a notarized affidavit from the arrested person or their representative within thirty (30) days of the date of this email, addressed to the attention of {{ user.title }} {{ user.last_name }} ({{ user.formatted_point_of_contact_number }}), who has been assigned to handle your case. Failure to do so will result in this file being CLOSED.

title: Denied - 50b

In order to release these records, this unit must receive a notarized affidavit from the victim, within thirty (30) days of the date of this email, addressed to the attention of {{ user.title }} {{ user.last_name }} ({{ user.formatted_point_of_contact_number }}), who has been assigned to handle your case. title: Denied - BWC Exemption
content:
Pursuant to an order by the Appellate Division First Judicial Department dated July 3, 2018, in the case of Patrolmen's Benevolent Association of the City of New York, Inc. v. Bill de Blasio in his official capacity as Mayor of the City of New York, James P. O'Neil in his official capacity as Commissioner of the City of New York Police Department, and New York City Police Department, Appelate Division of the Supreme Court of New York County, M-2522, M-2667, Index 150181/2018, the New York City Police Department is enjoined from releasing body camera footage absent compliance with N.Y. Civil Rights Law 50-a.

If there is a need for body camera footage while the stay is in effect, one should, on notice, petition either a Justice of the Supreme Court or the Appellate Division, requesting an order modifying the stay for a particular video(s).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(a) - State/Federal Statute
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied because the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they are specifically exempted from disclosure by state or federal statute. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(a).

title: Public Officers Law secs. 87(2)(b) and 89(2)(b) - Personal Privacy
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because disclosure would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. Public Officers Law secs. 87(2)(b) and 89(2)(b).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(c) - Contract Awards/Collective Bargaining
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they would impair present or imminent contract awards or collective bargaining negotiations. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(c).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(d) - Trade Secrets/Commercial Enterprise
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they are trade secrets or have been submitted by a commercial enterprise or derived from information obtained from a commercial enterprise and which if disclosed would cause substantial injury to the competitive position of the subject enterprise. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(d).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(i) - Law Enforcement/Judicial Proceedings
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they were compiled for law enforcement purposes and disclosure would interfere with law enforcement investigations or judicial proceedings. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(i).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(ii) - Fair Trial/Impartial Adjudication
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they were compiled for law enforcement purposes and disclosure would deprive a person of a right to a fair trial or impartial adjudication. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(ii).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(iii) - Confidential Source/Information
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they were compiled for law enforcement purposes and if disclosed would identify a confidential source or disclose confidential information relating to a criminal investigation. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(iii).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(iv) - Investigative Techniques/Procedures
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they were compiled for law enforcement purposes and would reveal criminal investigative techniques or procedures that are not routine. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(e)(iv).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(f) - Endanger Life/Safety
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because if disclosed the records could endanger the life or safety of any person. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(f).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(g) - Inter-Agency/Intra-Agency Materials
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they are inter-agency or intra-agency materials which are not: statistical or factual tabulations or data; instructions to staff that affect the public; final agency policy or determinations or external audits. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(g).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(h) - Examination Questions/Answers
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they are examination questions or answers that were requested prior to the final administration of the examination. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(h).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(i) - Information Technology Assets
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because if disclosed, the records would jeopardize the capacity of the agency or an entity that has shared information with the agency to guarantee the security of its information technology assets encompassing both electronic information systems and infrastructure. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(i).

title: Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(j)-(r) - Vehicle/Traffic Law
content: Your request under the Freedom of Information Law (FOIL) is denied on the ground that the records you requested are exempt from disclosure under FOIL because they are photographs, microphotographs, videotapes, or other recorded images prepared under the authority of the vehicle and traffic law. Public Officers Law sec. 87(2)(j)-(r).

title: Archived Records/Case Over 25 Years Old - JUD 89; POL 89(3)
content:
Our records indicate that the subject case files, which are over 25 years old, have been archived in accordance with City retention laws and are no longer in the possession of or maintained by this office. Judiciary Law § 89. FOIL does not “require any entity to prepare any record not possessed or maintained by such entity.” Public Officers Law § 89(3)(a).

You may wish to send your request directly to the NYC Department of Records & Information Services located at 31 Chambers Street, Suite 305, New York, NY 10007 or email your request to: research@records.nyc.gov. Additionally, please be advised that in order for them to identify and locate the requested case, you will need to provide the defendant’s name(s) or case number(s) in your request.

title: Attorney Work Product - POL 87(2)(a); CPLR 3101
content: Access is denied to records that which constitute attorney work-product, which is defined as material that encompasses mental impressions, opinions, legal theories, notes, instructions, directives, and internal communications of an attorney. Such records are intended solely for use within the office and are exempt from production. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a); Civil Practice Law & Rules § 3101.

title: Autopsy/Medical Examiner (OCME) Records - POL 87(2)(a); NYC Charter 557(g)
content: Access is denied to any records of the Office of Chief Medical Examiner (OCME), including autopsy reports and photographs, because they are exempt from FOIL disclosure under the City Charter. Public Officers Law §87(2)(a); NYC Charter § 557(g).

title: Cannot Locate Records/Arrest Voided by NYPD - POL 89(3)
content: Your request is necessarily denied as our system indicates the arrest was voided by the New York Police Department. As such, this agency does not possess paperwork connected to your request. Public Officers Law § 89(3)(a).

title: Cannot Locate Records/Not Required to Create a New Record - POL 89(3)
content: Your request is necessarily denied since the document you describe is not a record maintained by this office. Public Officers Law § 89(3)(a).

title: Cannot Locate Records/Records Not Reasonably Described in General - POL 89(3)
content: Your request for records is denied because we cannot locate or identify responsive records. Public Officers Law § 89(3)(a).

title: Cannot Locate Records/Records Not Reasonably Described/Requires Analysis or Speculation - POL 89(3)
content: Your request is denied because the request does not reasonably describe a record such that a Records Access Officer can identify the requested record without investigation, speculation or analysis. Public Officers Law (POL) § 89(3)(a).

title: Cannot Locate Records/Request for Information, Not Records - POL 89(3)
content: FOIL does not require an agency to provide information or answer questions, but only to make existing records available upon request where such records do not fall under one or more grounds for denial. Public Officers Law §89(3)(a).

title: Cannot Locate Records/Unable to Locate or Identify Case With Information Provided - POL 89(3)
content: Your request for records is denied because, despite conducting a diligent search with the information provided, we were unable to identify a case which matches the information you provided. Public Officers Law § 89(3)(a).

title: Department of Motor Vehicle Records - POL 87(2)(a)+(j)-(r); VTL 313(4)(h); 18 USC 2721(b)(1-14)
content: Access is denied to records prepared or maintained by the Department of Motor Vehicles as they are exempt from production under FOIL by statute. Public Officers Law §87(2)(a); Vehicle and Traffic Law § 313 (4)(h); 18 USC § 2721(b)(1-14).

title: Department of Social Services Records - POL 87(2)(a); SSL 422(4)(a)
content: Access is denied to Department of Social Services records pertaining to minors as they are confidential in nature and specifically exempted from disclosure by statute. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a); Social Services Law § 422(4)(a).

title: Education Records - POL 87(2)(a); FERPA 20 USC 1232g; 34 CFR Part 99
content: Access is denied to education records because they are confidential records whose disclosure is barred by statute. Public Officers Law §87(2)(a); Family Education Right and Privacy Act, 20 USC § 1232g; 34 Code of Federal Regulations Part 99.

title: Grand Jury Records - POL 87(2)(a); CPL 190.25(4)(a)
content: Access is denied to any materials attending a grand jury proceeding, including records obtained by grand jury subpoena and the subpoenas themselves, which are exempt to preserve the secrecy of the grand jury. Public Officers Law §87(2)(a); Criminal Procedure Law § 190.25(4)(a).

title: Intra-Agency Deliberative Material - POL 87(2)(g)
content: Access is denied to records that constitute deliberative intra-agency communications and that do not fall under one of the exceptions under Public Officers Law § 87(2)(g).

title: Law Enforcement/Rap Sheet & CJA - POL 87(2)(a)+(g)(i)-(iii), 89(2)(b)(viii); 9 NYCCRR 6150.4
content: Access is denied to “rap” sheets and the New York City Criminal Justice Agency interview reports because these constitute inter-agency records compiled solely for law enforcement purposes, that which are exempt from FOIL by statute. Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(a)+(g)(i)-(iii), 89(2)(b)(viii); 9 New York Codes, Rules and Regulations § 6150.4 (a)(b)(e)

title: Law Enforcement/Non-routine Techniques - POL 87(2)(e)(iv)
content: Access is denied to records which would reveal non-routine law enforcement and/or criminal investigative techniques, which are exempt from disclosure under FOIL pursuant to Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e)(iv).

title: Law Enforcement/Wiretaps - POL 87(2)(a)+(e); CPL 700.55 + 705.30; 18 USC 2703
content: Access is denied to any record pertaining to eavesdropping, wiretapping, pen registers, and trap/trace devices since such records are sealed by statute and only available pursuant to court order. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a)+(e); Criminal Procedure Law §§ 700.55, 705.30; Title 18 USC § 2703.

title: Law Enforcement/Confidential Sources - POL 87(2)(e)(iii)+(iv)
content: Access is denied any material compiled for law enforcement purposes that would identify a confidential source or disclose confidential information relating to an investigation. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e)(iii)+(iv).

title: Medical & Mental Health Records - POL 87(2)(a); CPLR 4504, 4507, 4508; MHL 33.13; PHL 2803-c(3)(f), 2805(g)
content: Access is denied to medical and mental health records because they are confidential records whose disclosure, absent a signed waiver from the subject of the records, would violate state statute and constitute an unwarranted invasion of privacy. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a)+(b); Civil Practice Law and Rules §§ 4504, 4507, 4508; Mental Hygiene Law § 33.13; Public Health Law §§ 2803-c(3)(f), 2805(g).

title: Mugshots & Booking Photos - POL 87(2)(b)+(g), 89(2)(b)(viii)
content: Access is denied to mugshots and/or booking photos because they are compiled for law enforcement purposes only and constitute inter-agency documents that are exempt from public disclosure under Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b)+(g); 89(2)(b)(viii).

title: Pending Proceedings/Appeal - POL 87(2)(e)(i)
content: Your request is denied because disclosure of records would interfere with a pending appeal, or with any further investigation and future judicial proceedings that might be necessary. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e)(i).

title: Pending Proceedings/General - POL 87(2)(e)(i)
content: Your request is denied because disclosure of records would interfere with a pending case, or with any further investigation and future judicial proceedings that might be necessary. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(e)(i).

title: Physical Evidence - Definition of a Record - POL 86(4)
content: Access is denied since physical evidence does not fall within the statutory definition of a “record” subject to FOIL. Public Officers Law § 86(4).

title: Presentencing Report - POL 87(2)(a); CPL 390.50; 9 NYCCRR 348.7
content: Access is denied to presentencing reports because they are confidential records which are specifically exempted from disclosure by statute. Public Officers Law §87 (2)(a); Criminal Procedure Law § 390.50; 9 New York Comp. Codes Rules and Regulations § 348.7.

title: Previously Provided
content: Your request for materials previously provided to you or your attorney is denied as FOIL does not require the District Attorney’s Office to provide duplicates of documents previously provided, absent a showing in evidentiary form that such documents are no longer available to you. See Matter of Huston v Turkel, 236 AD 2d 283 (1st Dept 1997).

title: Privacy/In General - POL (2)(b); POL 89(2)
content: Access is denied to records, the disclosure of which would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b) + 89(2)(b).

title: Privacy/Financial Records - POL 87(2)(b); POL 89(2)(b)(i)+(iv)
content: Access is denied to bank statements and other financial records as confidential records such that disclosure under FOIL would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b) + 89(2)(b)(i)+(iv).

title: Privacy/Graphic Content - POL 87(2)(b)
content: Access is denied to any graphic images or videos that, if disclosed, would constitute an unwarranted invasion of privacy. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(b).

title: Privacy/Witness Statements - POL 87(2)(b); POL 89(2)(b)
content: Access is denied to witness statements on privacy grounds. Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b); 89(2)(b).

title: Probation and Parole Records - POL 87(2)(a); EXC 259-k; CPL 390.50; 9 NYCCRR 348.7
content: Access is denied to probation and parole records because they are confidential court records only available by court order. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a)+(g); Executive Law § 259-k; Criminal Procedure Law § 390.50; 9 New York Comp. Codes Rules and Regulations § 8000.5.

title: Safety Grounds - POL 87(2)(f)
content: Access is denied to records that, if disclosed, could endanger the life and safety of any person. Public Officers Law §§ 87(2)(b)+(f).

title: Sealed Case - CPL 160.50
content: Your request is denied as the specified case is sealed pursuant to Criminal Procedure Law § 160.50. Absent a signed waiver from the defendant or a court order, our office is barred by state statute from accessing or releasing case paperwork.

title: Sealed Case - CPL 160.55
content: Your request is denied as the specified case is sealed pursuant to Criminal Procedure Law § 160.55. Absent a signed waiver from the defendant or a court order, our office is barred by state statute from accessing or releasing case paperwork.

title: Search Warrant Affidavits - POL 87(2)(a); CPL 690.55
content: Access is denied to search warrant applications because they are confidential records which are not available absent a court order. Public Officers Law § 87(2)(a); Criminal Procedure Law § 690.55.

title: Sex Offense Case - POL 87(2)(a); CRL 50-b
content: Access is denied to records which tend to identify the victim of a sex offense, since disclosure of such confidential records is strictly prohibited by statute. Public Officers Law §87(2)(a); Civil Rights Law § 50-b(1). Please feel free to resubmit your request with a Civil Rights Law § 50-b waiver signed by the victim for further consideration.
